title: contingency-dsl AST
description: Abstract Syntax Tree schema for contingency-dsl v1.0. Language-independent; used by conformance test suites.

program_annotations.description: Session-wide annotation defaults. Appear before param_decls in source. Schedule-level annotations override these for the attached expression. Optional field: absent ≡ empty array (unannotated program).

Annotation.description: A single annotation instance. Keyword is defined by the annotator that owns it.

positional.description: First positional argument (string or number), if any.

params.description: Named parameters (key=value pairs). Time values (e.g., time=60min in source) are normalized to a number in the canonical unit (seconds by default) before storage; the original unit is not preserved in the AST.

ParamDecl.description: Program-level parameter declaration. LH applies to all leaf schedules. COD/FRCO apply to all Conc combinators. BO applies to all Mult/Mix combinators. Expression-level overrides program-level.

Atomic.description: Parametric atomic schedule: dist × domain with a numeric value. Examples: FR5, VI60s, RT20ms

Special.description: Boundary schedules with no parameters: EXT (zero element) or CRF (identity = FR1).

Compound.description: Compound schedule: combinator applied to 2+ sub-schedules, with optional keyword parameters. v1.1: COD and FRCO for Conc, BO for Mult/Mix.

CombinatorParams.description: Keyword arguments for compound schedule combinators. COD/FRCO: Conc-only. BO: Mult/Mix-only. Semantic pass validates per-combinator validity.

COD.description: Changeover Delay: minimum time after operandum switch before reinforcement is available (Catania, 1966). Conc-only, mandatory.

FRCO.description: Fixed-Ratio Changeover: number of responses required on new operandum before reinforcement is available (Hunter & Davison, 1985; Pliskoff & Fetterman, 1981). Conc-only, optional.

BO.description: Blackout: response-independent dark period between component transitions (Reynolds, 1961). Mult/Mix-only, optional. Omission = no blackout (immediate transition).

Modifier.description: Modifier schedule: DR (DRL/DRH/DRO with value), PR (with step params), Repeat (with count + inner), Lag (Page & Neuringer, 1985), or Pctl (Core-Stateful: Platt, 1973; Galbicka, 1994).

length.description: Sequence length for Lag schedule (Page & Neuringer, 1985). Omitted or set to 1 means individual response is the unit (applied research default). Set to N means the unit is a sequence of N responses (e.g., Page & Neuringer used length=8 for 2-key 8-peck sequences). Only applicable when modifier == 'Lag'.

pctl_target.description: Core-Stateful: Response dimension to measure (Platt, 1973). IRT: inter-response time. latency: response latency. duration: response duration. force: response force. rate: local response rate. Only applicable when modifier == 'Pctl'.

pctl_rank.description: Core-Stateful: Percentile threshold (0–100). Only applicable when modifier == 'Pctl'.

pctl_window.description: Core-Stateful: Number of recent responses for distribution calculation. Default: 20. Only applicable when modifier == 'Pctl'.

pctl_dir.description: Core-Stateful: Reinforcement direction. 'below': reinforce at or below threshold. 'above': reinforce at or above. Default: 'below'. Only applicable when modifier == 'Pctl'.

LimitedHold.description: Temporal availability window. FI30 LH10 = FI 30s with 10s hold.

SecondOrder.description: Second-order schedule. FR5(FI30) = 5 completions of FI30 produce reinforcement. Overall must be Atomic (no EXT/CRF). Unit must be simple schedule (v1.0).

AversiveSchedule.description: Aversive schedule primitive. v1.x introduces Sidman free-operant avoidance (Sidman, 1953) and discriminated avoidance (Solomon & Wynne, 1953). The contingency structure differs from the F/V/R × R/I/T reinforcement matrix and requires a dedicated node type.

SidmanParams.description: Parameters for Sidman free-operant avoidance (Sidman, 1953). Both SSI and RSI are mandatory; no defaults. Formally: next_shock(t) = max(last_shock + SSI, last_response + RSI).

SSI.description: Shock-Shock Interval: baseline time between shocks in absence of response. Must be positive; time unit required.

RSI.description: Response-Shock Interval: postponement time triggered by each response. Must be positive; time unit required. Linter warns if RSI > SSI.

DiscrimAvParams.description: Parameters for discriminated avoidance (Solomon & Wynne, 1953). CSUSInterval, ITI, and mode are mandatory. ShockDuration is required for mode=fixed. MaxShock is optional for mode=escape.

CSUSInterval.description: CS-US Interval: time from CS onset to US onset. Must be positive; time unit required.

ITI.description: Inter-Trial Interval: CS-onset to next CS-onset. Must be positive and > CSUSInterval; time unit required.

mode.description: US delivery mode. 'fixed': US presented for a fixed ShockDuration. 'escape': US terminated by response (optional MaxShock safety cutoff).

ShockDuration.description: Duration of the fixed US presentation. Required when mode=fixed; forbidden when mode=escape.

MaxShock.description: Safety cutoff for escape-terminated US (Solomon & Wynne, 1953 used 2min). Optional when mode=escape; forbidden when mode=fixed. Null/absent = no cutoff.

IdentifierRef.description: Reference to a let-bound name. Present only in pre-expansion AST; removed after semantic analysis.

TimeUnit.description: Declared time unit. null = dimensionless (ratio schedules).
